column1: # Per-cell background color from an xlsx import paints through to the
# rendered terminal buffer.  A1..C1 carry explicit solid fills in the
# fixture; D1 is plain. The pointer cell should suppress its fill so
# the REVERSED highlight stays legible, but non-pointer cells with
# fills should show the exact RGB.
# Sanity: content survived the round trip.
# Pointer sits on A1 by default; its fill is suppressed so the REVERSED
# highlight is still legible.  Drive the pointer away to expose A1's
# paint, then verify each fill cell.
# Park the pointer on a filled cell — the fill should suppress back to
# the terminal default so the REVERSED highlight stays loud.  Note the
# cell's *trimmed* text is still "green" (ASSERT_CELL trims whitespace).
# Adjacent cells keep their fills (pointer-suppression is per-cell).
